instruction_set.title: Real Corpus, Provenance-Aware Retrieval, and Adversarial Evaluation

metadata.author_context: The project is a neuroscience-focused search and discovery engine designed to connect datasets, papers, experimental paradigms, modalities, brain regions, behavioral labels, analysis goals, and eventually future-study design recommendations.
metadata.strategic_warning: The current benchmark score is high, but may reflect co-evolution between the curated demo corpus, ontology labels, and benchmark queries. Treat the v0.2 benchmark as a regression suite, not as the main scientific validity signal.
metadata.north_star: Build Neural Search into a scientifically credible retrieval system that can ingest real neuroscience datasets and papers, preserve provenance for every extracted label, rank results by interpretable scoring heads, and evaluate against real and adversarial scientific queries.

global_rules.rule: Do not optimize further against the existing v0.2 demo benchmark.
Freeze the current benchmark as a regression suite.
Do not prioritize frontend, design, dashboards, or demo polish in v0.3.
Focus on search quality, ingestion robustness, provenance, scoring explainability, and adversarial evaluation.
Every extracted scientific label must eventually include confidence and evidence provenance.
All new behavior must be covered by tests or fixture-based validation scripts.
Prefer small composable modules over large monolithic scripts.
Prefer deterministic, inspectable behavior before introducing learned models.
Preserve backward compatibility with existing v0.2 APIs unless impossible.
Do not delete existing v0.2 reports or benchmark outputs; version them.
Use fixture payloads for ingestion tests so CI does not depend on live APIs.
When adding optional dependencies, make them extras and do not break minimal install.

target_release.name: v0.3
target_release.goal: Convert Neural Search from a curated demo retrieval engine into a provenance-aware real-corpus neuroscience search system with adversarial evaluation and interpretable ranking.

non_goals.item: Do not build a polished frontend.
Do not build investor demo slides.
Do not tune the existing 30 benchmark queries.
Do not train a large neural foundation model yet.
Do not add cosmetic synthetic datasets just to improve reported metrics.

definition_of_done.item: The v0.2 demo benchmark is frozen under versioned filenames and still runnable.
Benchmark runner supports suite selection: demo_v02, real_corpus, adversarial.
At least 100 real external records can be normalized or dry-run normalized from DANDI/OpenNeuro/OpenAlex.
Normalized records include stable IDs, source URLs, raw payload references, extracted labels, confidence, and evidence.
Corpus QA reports are generated from real or fixture-backed ingested records.
Retrieval results expose score decomposition across multiple interpretable scoring heads.
Search supports negative constraints and explains excluded or penalized matches.
Tests cover benchmark versioning, ingestion normalization, provenance labels, corpus reports, scoring decomposition, and hard negatives.
Quality gates pass locally: pytest, ruff, benchmark runner, corpus report generation.

workstream.title: Architecture Audit and v0.3 Design Lock
workstream.objective: Inspect the existing repository and produce a short implementation plan that maps existing modules to v0.3 responsibilities before changing code.

tasks.task: Identify current package structure and entry points.
Identify current benchmark query files, report outputs, and evaluation runner.
Identify current ingestion modules for DANDI, OpenNeuro, and OpenAlex.
Identify current schema/dataclass/model definitions for datasets, labels, papers, and results.
Identify current retrieval scoring code and latent search prototype.
Write docs/V0_3_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md with concrete file-level changes.

acceptance_criteria.item: Plan separates must-do v0.3 work from later v0.4/v0.5 work.
Plan identifies files to modify and files to leave alone.
Plan explicitly preserves v0.2 benchmark as regression-only.

workstream.title: Versioned Benchmark Suites
workstream.objective: Refactor benchmark handling so v0.2 remains frozen while new real-corpus and adversarial benchmark suites can be added safely.

tasks.task: Create explicit benchmark suite names: demo_v02, real_corpus, adversarial.
Update benchmark runner to accept --suite demo_v02|real_corpus|adversarial|all.
Make report output suite-specific.
Ensure old benchmark queries are copied or migrated without semantic changes.
Add placeholder real_corpus and adversarial benchmark files with schema-valid examples.
Add tests for suite selection and output paths.

acceptance_criteria.item: Existing v0.2 benchmark behavior is preserved.
Benchmark suite selection is tested.
Report files do not overwrite each other across suites.

workstream.title: Provenance-Aware Normalized Corpus Schema
workstream.objective: Introduce a schema for normalized dataset and paper records where extracted scientific labels carry confidence, evidence, and extractor provenance.

tasks.task: Add or extend schema module for normalized records.
Add EvidenceLabel with strict validation that confidence is between 0 and 1.
Add extractor version constants.
Add serialization to JSON and JSONL.
Add stable ID generation helpers.
Add tests for schema validation and serialization roundtrips.

acceptance_criteria.item: Every normalized extracted label can carry evidence and confidence.
Schema supports both datasets and papers.
Roundtrip serialization tests pass.

workstream.title: Real Source Ingestion Hardening
workstream.objective: Make DANDI, OpenNeuro, and OpenAlex ingestion robust enough to normalize batches of real records into the new schema while preserving raw payloads.

tasks.task: Audit existing DANDI ingestion and map fields to NormalizedDatasetRecord.
Audit existing OpenNeuro ingestion and map fields to NormalizedDatasetRecord.
Audit existing OpenAlex ingestion and map fields to NormalizedPaperRecord.
Add --dry-run mode to each ingestion CLI if missing.
Add --save-raw mode to preserve raw API payloads.
Add --limit argument and deterministic pagination behavior.
Add deduplication by source/source_id and stable normalized ID.
Add fixture payloads for representative DANDI/OpenNeuro/OpenAlex records.
Add tests that normalize fixture payloads without network access.

recommended_commands.command: python -m neural_search.ingestion.openalex --query "reversal learning electrophysiology orbitofrontal cortex" --limit 50 --dry-run --save-raw
python -m neural_search.ingestion.openalex --query "motor imagery EEG BCI classification" --limit 50 --dry-run --save-raw

acceptance_criteria.item: At least one fixture-backed test exists per source.
Raw payloads and normalized payloads are both preserved.
Missing metadata does not crash normalization.
Malformed records are logged with errors rather than silently dropped.

workstream.title: Scientific Label Extraction Rules
workstream.objective: Improve extraction quality for neuroscience entities while preserving explainability and avoiding unsupported label hallucination.

tasks.task: Expand ontology mapping only when terms are grounded in real source metadata or known neuroscience vocabulary.
Add extractor rules that return EvidenceLabel objects instead of plain strings.
Use confidence tiers: exact metadata match, synonym match, description phrase match, weak inference.
Do not infer behavioral tasks from a brain region alone.
Do not infer analysis support unless required fields or strong metadata evidence exist.
Add tests for common synonyms and false positives.

confidence_guidance.tier: Label appears in structured metadata field or controlled vocabulary.
Label appears through a curated synonym or common abbreviation.
Label appears in free text description or abstract.
Label is inferred indirectly and must expose evidence and uncertainty.
Do not include label unless needed as a rejected candidate in debug output.

acceptance_criteria.item: Labels expose evidence text and source field.
False positive tests exist for common traps.
Weak inferences are visibly lower confidence.

workstream.title: Corpus QA Reports
workstream.objective: Generate inspectable reports that show what the corpus contains, what is missing, and where label extraction is uncertain.

tasks.task: Add a corpus report generator CLI.
Count records by source.
Count modalities, species, tasks, brain regions, behavior labels, and data standards.
Report missing fields by source.
List low-confidence extracted labels.
List candidate dataset-paper links and their evidence.
Add tests using a tiny fixture corpus.

acceptance_criteria.item: Reports run without requiring frontend or notebooks.
Reports are deterministic for a fixed input corpus.
Reports expose missingness and uncertainty rather than hiding it.

workstream.title: Interpretable Multi-Head Scoring
workstream.objective: Refactor ranking so every result exposes an interpretable score decomposition instead of one opaque score.

score_heads.score_head: Task, behavior, modality, region, species, and standard matching.
Source quality, evidence confidence, linked papers, metadata traceability.
Trials, events, behavior, neural data, standard formats, analysis readiness.
Whether record supports the requested analysis goal.
Penalty for excluded modality/species/task/region or missing required conditions.

tasks.task: Introduce ScoreBreakdown model.
Refactor existing scoring into separable functions or classes.
Add default weighting config.
Add query intent/constraint parser if needed.
Ensure NOT/exclusion logic feeds negative_constraint_score.
Add tests for each scoring head.
Add ablation-style debug output for top results.

acceptance_criteria.item: Top-k results include score_breakdown.
Negative constraints are visible, not silently applied.
Tests prove that excluded modalities/tasks are penalized or filtered.

workstream.title: Adversarial Scientific Benchmark Design
workstream.objective: Create benchmark queries that test scientific reasoning, ambiguity handling, exclusions, and analysis affordance matching.

example_queries.query: Find OFC reversal learning datasets, but exclude fMRI and pure behavior-only studies.
Find datasets suitable for fitting Q-learning models where choices, rewards, and trial outcomes are explicitly available.
Find motor cortex reaching datasets with continuous kinematics, not just event labels.
Find human speech ECoG datasets that include auditory feedback or articulation events.
Find calcium imaging datasets that could support trial-aligned activity analysis but are missing explicit lick labels.
Find datasets related to dopamine reward prediction error but not seizure monitoring, sleep, or auditory tasks.
Find visual decision-making datasets with Neuropixels recordings and stimulus timing.
Find datasets that are probably useful for BCI decoding but lack continuous behavioral labels.

tasks.task: Create at least 30 adversarial benchmark queries initially.
Each query should include expected positives, expected negatives, and rationale where known.
Do not require all queries to pass at first; this benchmark should reveal gaps.
Add metric categories: pass/fail, precision@k, label recall, exclusion correctness, missingness awareness.
Add docs explaining why each adversarial query matters scientifically.

acceptance_criteria.item: Adversarial benchmark includes hard negatives.
Benchmark can record failure modes without being treated as build failure initially.
Queries test scientific usability, not just term matching.

workstream.title: Analysis Affordance Detection
workstream.objective: Estimate which scientific analyses each dataset can support based on metadata, labels, and file structure hints.

tasks.task: Add AnalysisAffordance model.
Add rule-based affordance detector.
Attach affordance results to normalized dataset records or derived report output.
Make analysis_fit_score consume affordance results.
Add tests for high/medium/low support cases.

acceptance_criteria.item: Search can explain whether a dataset supports a requested analysis.
Missing required fields are exposed.
Affordance detection is conservative and evidence-based.

workstream.title: Latent Search Roadmap Without Premature Model Training
workstream.objective: Keep latent search alive, but redirect v0.3 toward deterministic scientific fingerprints before training learned latent models.

tasks.task: Audit current hybrid_search_with_latent API.
Document current limitations of hashing or placeholder embeddings.
Define deterministic session/dataset fingerprint fields for NWB and BIDS.
Do not train a large model in v0.3.
Prepare interfaces so v0.4/v0.5 can add learned embeddings cleanly.

acceptance_criteria.item: Latent search remains compatible with future learned models.
v0.3 does not depend on expensive cloud training.
Dataset similarity can be improved through deterministic fingerprints first.

phase.name: Reports and Scoring
phase.step: Run corpus report generation and benchmark demo_v02.

phase.name: Scientific Evaluation
phase.step: Claude completes WS8 adversarial benchmark design.
Codex wires benchmark metrics for exclusion correctness and missingness awareness.
Run demo_v02, real_corpus, and adversarial benchmark suites.

phase.name: Latent Foundation Preparation
phase.step: Codex optionally adds deterministic fingerprint interfaces if prior phases are stable.

fallbacks.fallback: If mypy is already used in the repo, run it and keep it passing.
If optional dependencies are missing, document them and add extras rather than breaking base install.
If live API access fails, use saved fixture payloads and dry-run normalization.

prompt.title: Claude Master Prompt for Neural Search v0.3

handoff_prompts.prompt:
You are working on Neural Search v0.3.
Context: Neural Search v0.2 is complete with a curated 26-dataset demo corpus, expanded ontology, 29/30 benchmark pass rate, NOT exclusion parsing, and a prototype hybrid_search_with_latent API.
Your mission: Do not tune the existing v0.2 benchmark. Freeze it as a regression suite. Design and help implement v0.3, focused on real corpus ingestion, provenance-aware labels, adversarial scientific evaluation, interpretable scoring, and analysis-affordance detection.
Primary responsibilities: 1. Audit the repo and write docs/V0_3_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md. 2. Create docs/V0_3_RISK_REGISTER.md. 3. Improve scientific label extraction rules so labels include confidence and evidence. 4. Design adversarial benchmark queries that test real scientific retrieval, hard negatives, and missing metadata awareness. 5. Keep latent search pointed toward deterministic dataset/session fingerprints before expensive learned model training. 6. Avoid frontend work. 7. Avoid cosmetic synthetic data expansion. 8. Preserve v0.2 benchmark behavior.
Concrete deliverables: - docs/V0_3_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md - docs/V0_3_RISK_REGISTER.md - improved extraction rule docs or code changes - data/eval/benchmark_queries_adversarial.yaml - docs/LATENT_SEARCH_V0_3_ROADMAP.md
Definition of done: - v0.2 benchmark is frozen and still runnable. - v0.3 benchmark design includes real-corpus and adversarial tiers. - Adversarial queries include expected positives, expected negatives, and scientific rationale where possible. - Label extraction is conservative, evidence-based, and confidence-aware. - No frontend work is prioritized.

prompt.title: Codex Backend Execution Prompt for Neural Search v0.3

handoff_prompts.prompt:
You are working on Neural Search v0.3.
Context: The repo has a completed v0.2 state with curated demo data, expanded ontology, strong benchmark performance, and a prototype latent search path. The next milestone is not frontend polish. It is real corpus ingestion, provenance-aware normalized records, benchmark versioning, scoring decomposition, and corpus QA reporting.
Your mission: Implement the backend/search/data infrastructure for Neural Search v0.3 while preserving v0.2 behavior.
Required implementation areas: 1. Versioned benchmark suites: - demo_v02 - real_corpus - adversarial - benchmark runner accepts --suite - reports are written to suite-specific result directories
2. Provenance-aware schema: - NormalizedDatasetRecord - NormalizedPaperRecord - EvidenceLabel - UsabilityFlags - AnalysisAffordance if appropriate - JSON/JSONL serialization and roundtrip tests
3. Ingestion hardening: - DANDI normalization - OpenNeuro normalization - OpenAlex normalization - --dry-run - --save-raw - --limit - stable IDs - deduplication - fixture-backed tests
4. Corpus QA reports: - corpus coverage - missing metadata - label confidence - source distribution - dataset-paper linking candidates - low-confidence labels
5. Interpretable scoring: - lexical_score - ontology_score - semantic_score - provenance_score - usability_score - analysis_fit_score - negative_constraint_score - final_score - visible result explanations
6. Tests: - benchmark suite selection - ingestion normalization fixtures - evidence label validation - corpus report generation - score breakdown correctness - hard negative behavior
Do not: - tune the old benchmark - add cosmetic synthetic datasets - build frontend features - train a large latent model - silently infer labels without evidence - overwrite v0.2 reports
Quality gates: Run: python -m pip install -e ".[dev]" pytest -q ruff check . python -m neural_search.evaluation.run_benchmark --suite demo_v02 python -m neural_search.evaluation.run_benchmark --suite real_corpus python -m neural_search.evaluation.run_benchmark --suite adversarial python -m neural_search.reports.corpus_report --input data/corpus/normalized --out data/reports
If commands differ because the repo already uses different entry points, preserve the existing convention and document the actual commands in docs/V0_3_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md.

recommended_git_branches.branch: Scientific adversarial benchmark design and metrics.

suggested_first_commit_plan.commit: Freeze v0.2 benchmark files and add suite names.
Add normalized schema models and serialization tests.
Add fixture payloads and ingestion normalization tests.
Add corpus report generator and tiny fixture corpus.
Refactor score breakdown model and result explanations.
Add adversarial benchmark skeleton and metrics.
Run quality gates and write v0.3 alpha report.

items.item: Add BM25 if not already present.

v1_0_direction.description: The long-term system should search datasets and papers, compare experiments, identify missing controls, recommend analyses, discover gaps, and propose future experiments grounded in real provenance.
